papers.title: Beyond the structure-function paradigm: A comprehensive review of intrinsically disordered proteins.
papers.abstract: Intrinsically disordered proteins (IDPs) and regions (IDRs) challenge the classical structure-function paradigm by fulfilling essential biological roles in the absence of a stable three-dimensional fold. Rather than occupying fixed conformations, IDPs exist as dynamic ensembles that enable high-specificity, low-affinity interactions, multivalent regulatory functions, and context-dependent binding across diverse cellular environments. This conformational plasticity underlies their central roles in signaling, transcriptional regulation, chromatin organization, and the assembly of membrane-less o
papers.summary: This review explains how intrinsically disordered proteins (IDPs) function without a fixed 3D structure, using their dynamic, flexible nature to perform key roles in cell signaling, gene regulation, and forming membrane-less organelles. It highlights how this challenges the traditional view that protein function requires a stable folded shape.

papers.title: RdRpCATCH: a unified resource for RNA virus discovery using viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase profile Hidden Markov models.
papers.abstract: Recent advances in large-scale sequence mining have expanded our knowledge of RNA virus diversity. Most genome mining approaches for detecting RNA viruses rely on identifying the conserved RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) by scanning sequencing datasets with specialized profile Hidden Markov Models (pHMMs). Recently, several new pHMM databases for RdRp detection have been released, each following distinct design principles. However, their relative performance remains unclear, and their accessibility to users without advanced computational expertise is limited. Here, we introduce the RdRp Co
papers.summary: This paper presents RdRpCATCH, a unified resource that combines multiple profile Hidden Markov Model databases for detecting RNA viruses via their RNA-dependent RNA polymerase. The tool makes RNA virus discovery more accessible to researchers without advanced computational skills by providing a user-friendly interface and benchmarking the performance of different models.

papers.title: Endoplasmic reticulum stress promotes colorectal cancer proliferation by regulating autophagy via XBP1s.
papers.abstract: XBP1s, a key downstream molecule of endoplasmic reticulum stress (ERS), is widely involved in processes such as proliferation, invasion, and apoptosis in cancer. However, the role and underlying mechanism of XBP1s in colorectal cancer (CRC) remain poorly understood. We hypothesize that XBP1s plays a critical role in promoting CRC proliferation through the regulation of autophagy. Therefore, this study aims to investigate the expression, biological functions, and underlying mechanisms of XBP1s in colorectal cancer, opening up new directions for targeted tumor therapy.
papers.summary: This study investigates the role of XBP1s, a molecule activated by endoplasmic reticulum stress, in colorectal cancer. The authors hypothesize that XBP1s promotes cancer cell proliferation by regulating autophagy, a cellular recycling process. The findings suggest that targeting this pathway could offer a new therapeutic strategy for colorectal cancer.

papers.title: An integrated multiomics-based risk model for predicting the prognosis and immune microenvironment status in patients with papillary thyroid carcinoma.
papers.abstract: Papillary thyroid carcinoma (PTC) is the most common type of primary endocrine malignancy. The tumor immune microenvironment (TIME) and genetic alterations play crucial roles in the progression of PTC. With the advance in research, there has been a heightened focus on re-evaluating molecular targeted therapies and identifying novel targets through molecular biology-based approaches. This study aimed to identify robust prognostic biomarkers and to construct a reliable risk model for patients with PTC by integrating multiomics data.
papers.summary: This study developed a risk model for papillary thyroid carcinoma by integrating multiomics data, including genetic and immune microenvironment features. The model predicts patient prognosis and immune status, potentially guiding personalized treatment decisions. It highlights the value of combining different molecular data types for cancer prognosis.

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence in Inherited Epidermolysis Bullosa: Current Evidence, Challenges, and Future Directions.
papers.abstract: Epidermolysis bullosa (EB) comprises a group of rare inherited genodermatoses characterized by fragility and blistering of the skin and mucous membranes, chronic wounding, and significant morbidity including increased risk of squamous cell carcinoma in severe subtypes. Key unmet priorities include reducing diagnostic latency, establishing objective wound monitoring, enabling early detection of malignant transformation within chronic ulcerations, and developing therapies that durably modify disease progression. Artificial intelligence (AI) encompassing machine learning (ML), and deep learning (
papers.summary: This review examines the potential of artificial intelligence (AI), including machine learning and deep learning, to address key challenges in inherited epidermolysis bullosa (EB), such as reducing diagnostic delays, improving wound monitoring, and enabling early cancer detection. The authors discuss current evidence, highlight limitations like data scarcity and model interpretability, and outline future directions for AI applications in this rare disease.

papers.title: High-fidelity rare structural variant detection with HiFiRE3 reduced representation via restriction enzyme ends.
papers.abstract: High-fidelity detection of rare structural variants (SVs) remains challenging because library preparation and sequencing techniques generate artifactual junctions that obscure true single-molecule events. Here, we present HiFiRe3, an error-minimized sequencing framework that combines artifact-aware library design with error suppression and correction strategies to enable rare SV detection and frequency assessment across long and short-read sequencing platforms. We first systematically characterized major classes of SV artifacts, including chimeric PCR products, intermolecular ligation, sequenc
papers.journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
papers.summary: This paper introduces HiFiRe3, a sequencing framework that reduces artifacts in structural variant detection by combining artifact-aware library design with error suppression strategies. The method enables reliable detection of rare structural variants across both long and short-read platforms.

papers.title: Genome-Wide Characterization and Light-Responsive Expression Patterns of B-Box Transcription Factors in Artemisia argyi.
papers.abstract: For over 3000 years, the perennial herb mugwort (Artemisia argyi) has served as a cornerstone of traditional Asian medicine. Its clinical efficacy is driven by a diverse array of specialized metabolites, most notably flavonoids and volatile oils. While B-box (BBX) transcription factors are known to dictate photomorphogenic development and secondary metabolic pathways in plants, this specific gene family has not yet been systematically analyzed in A. argyi. Leveraging a chromosome-level genomic assembly, we comprehensively identified and analyzed the complete repertoire of AarBBX genes, profili
papers.summary: This study systematically identified and characterized B-box (BBX) transcription factors in the medicinal plant Artemisia argyi (mugwort) using a chromosome-level genome assembly. The authors analyzed the expression patterns of these genes in response to light, providing insights into how they may regulate photomorphogenic development and the production of medicinal compounds like flavonoids.

papers.title: Genetic Diversity, Population Structure, Integration of Genome-Wide Association Studies and Machine Learning for Antibacterial Trait Analysis in the Mediterranean Spice Laurel (Laurus nobilis).
papers.abstract: Laural (Laurus nobilis) is a Mediterranean plant with reported antibacterial properties, yet the genetic basis of its antibacterial efficacy remains largely unexplored. This study evaluated the antibacterial activity of Laurus nobilis methanolic extracts against Escherichia coli, Staphylococcus aureus, and Bacillus cereus, combined with genome-wide association studies (GWAS) and machine learning (ML) approaches to identify genetic markers and predict antibacterial efficacy in 92 plant samples. Antibacterial tests revealed significant variability in inhibition zones, with E. coli showing the hi
papers.summary: This study combined genome-wide association studies (GWAS) and machine learning to identify genetic markers linked to antibacterial activity in 92 samples of the Mediterranean spice laurel. The researchers found significant variability in the plant's ability to inhibit E. coli, S. aureus, and B. cereus, suggesting a genetic basis for these traits.

papers.title: Long-read sequencing of single cell-derived melanoma sublines reveals divergent and parallel genomic and epigenomic evolutionary trajectories.
papers.abstract: Tumor evolution is driven by various mutational processes, ranging from single-nucleotide variants (SNVs) to large structural variants (SVs) to dynamic shifts in DNA methylation. Current short-read sequencing methods struggle to accurately capture the full spectrum of these genomic and epigenomic alterations due to inherent technical limitations. To overcome that, here we introduce an approach to identify and analyze the genomic and epigenetic events in different stages of tumoral evolution from long-read sequencing of single-cell derived sublines. We then use it to profile 23 sublines of a mo
papers.summary: This study introduces a long-read sequencing approach to analyze genomic and epigenetic evolution in single-cell derived melanoma sublines, overcoming short-read limitations. By profiling 23 sublines, it reveals both divergent and parallel evolutionary trajectories driven by structural variants and DNA methylation changes. The method provides a more complete picture of tumor evolution by capturing the full spectrum of mutational processes.

papers.title: TE_Bench: a foundational benchmarking workflow for transposable element annotation pipelines.
papers.abstract: Methods for computational discovery of transposable elements (TEs) in DNA sequences are under continual development. Although many TE annotation pipelines have been published, differences in their methods are often opaque and can lead to inconsistent results. As new methods emerge, there is a growing need for an informative and reproducible strategy to evaluate pipeline performance.
papers.summary: This paper introduces TE_Bench, a standardized benchmarking workflow designed to evaluate and compare different computational pipelines for identifying transposable elements (TEs) in DNA sequences. The tool addresses the lack of reproducibility and transparency in TE annotation by providing a consistent framework for performance assessment. This will help researchers choose the most appropriate pipeline for their specific needs and improve the reliability of TE studies.

papers.title: Advancing the diagnosis of rare neuromuscular and neurological diseases through the collaborative Solve-RD research framework.
papers.abstract: Rare neuromuscular and neurological diseases (NMDs and RNDs) present diagnostic challenges due to their clinical heterogeneity and genetic complexity. Despite the advancements in next-generation sequencing (NGS) and other high-throughput genomic technologies, a significant proportion of patients with NMDs and RNDs remain undiagnosed. This is primarily due to genetic heterogeneity, the presence of novel or private variants, and incomplete variant detection by short-read sequencing platforms. The Solve-RD project, a pan-European initiative funded by the Horizon 2020 programme, established a robu
papers.summary: The Solve-RD project, a large European collaboration, developed a robust framework to improve diagnosis of rare neuromuscular and neurological diseases by integrating advanced genomic technologies and data sharing. This initiative addresses the challenge of many patients remaining undiagnosed due to genetic complexity and limitations of standard sequencing methods.

papers.title: New Iflavirus identified in Chiroxiphia pareola birds from an ecotone area in northeast Brazil.
papers.abstract: Avian species play a key role in the ecology of viruses, acting as reservoirs, sentinels, and biological carriers across diverse environments. Here, we describe the detection and genomic characterization of a novel iflavirus identified in fecal samples from the Chiroxiphiapareola("blue-backed manakin") collected in areas of the Middle North region of Brazil, Maranhão state. Viral RNA was extracted from pool fecal samples and subjected to next-generation sequencing. De novo assembly and comparative analyses enabled the recovery of a complete picorna-like viral genome of 9,043 nucleotides, compr
papers.summary: Researchers discovered a new iflavirus in fecal samples from blue-backed manakin birds in Brazil, sequencing its complete genome of 9,043 nucleotides. This highlights the role of wild birds as reservoirs for novel viruses and expands knowledge of viral diversity in avian species.

papers.title: Integrative multi-omics quantitative trait loci prioritize CASP7 as a candidate protective gene for cataract.
papers.abstract: Cataracts are the leading cause of vision loss worldwide. Despite surgery being the only effective treatment, its economic burden highlights the necessity of exploring the pathogenesis of cataracts. In this study, we analyzed 4 large-scale GWAS (genome-wide association study) datasets for cataracts and performed SMR analysis along with heterogeneity in dependent instruments (HEIDI) testing to explore the effects of methylation, expression, and protein QTLs on cataracts. We further validated shared genetic variants through COLOC analysis. Additionally, we searched datasets related to cataracts
papers.summary: This study integrated multi-omics quantitative trait loci (QTL) data with large-scale GWAS to identify CASP7 as a potential protective gene for cataract. By analyzing methylation, expression, and protein QTLs, the authors found that CASP7 may influence cataract risk through epigenetic and transcriptional regulation. The findings suggest a novel candidate pathway for cataract pathogenesis, though further validation is needed.

papers.title: In vitro assays, probiogenomic analysis, safety assessment and probiotic potential of Enterococcus faecium F25.
papers.abstract: The probiogenomic approach has gained considerable attention for elucidating the genetic basis of probiotic functionality. In this study, the probiotic potential of Enterococcus faecium F25 was comprehensively evaluated using an integrated strategy combining traditional biochemical characterization, in vitro functional assays, and whole-genome-based analyses. The in vitro assessments were conducted using plate-based assays, while genomic investigations were performed using publicly available bioinformatic tools and platforms. Both approaches consistently confirmed the strain's typical enteroco
papers.summary: This study evaluates the probiotic potential of Enterococcus faecium F25 by combining traditional lab tests with whole-genome analysis, confirming its safety and functional properties. The integrated approach demonstrates how genomic data can support and extend conventional probiotic characterization methods.

papers.title: Panorama: A robust pangenome-based method for predicting and comparing biological systems across species.
papers.abstract: Over the last decade, the expansion in the number of available genomes has profoundly transformed the study of genetic diversity, evolution, and ecological adaptation in prokaryotes. However, traditional bioinformatic approaches based on the analysis of individual genomes are showing their limitations when faced with the sheer scale of the data. To overcome these constraints, the concept of pangenome has emerged, offering a comprehensive framework to capture the full genetic repertoire of a species. In this study, we present PANORAMA, an innovative pangenomic tool designed to exploit pangenome
papers.summary: PANORAMA is a new computational tool that uses pangenome data to predict and compare biological systems across prokaryotic species, overcoming limitations of traditional single-genome analyses. It leverages the full genetic repertoire of a species to enable robust cross-species comparisons.

papers.title: Exploring life's hidden majority: microbial dark matter symposium highlights.
papers.abstract: The Microbial Dark Matter Symposium held on August 28-29, 2025, in Laguna Beach, Orange County, CA, convened a multidisciplinary group of scientists to address the vast unknowns in microbial life-from uncultured taxa and uncharacterized proteins to elusive viruses and spacefaring microbes. Set against a scenic coastal backdrop, the symposium highlighted advances in single-cell genomics, proximity ligation sequencing, and artificial intelligence-ready bioinformatics, while also probing the limits of microbial persistence, metabolism, and ecological distribution. Sessions explored microbial dark
papers.summary: This report summarizes the Microbial Dark Matter Symposium, which brought together scientists to discuss the vast unknowns in microbiology, including uncultured taxa, uncharacterized proteins, and elusive viruses. Key advances highlighted include single-cell genomics, proximity ligation sequencing, and AI-ready bioinformatics for exploring microbial persistence and ecology.

papers.title: nf-core/magmap: Map metatranscriptomes to large collections of genomes.
papers.abstract: The lack of publicly available reference genomes has forced annotation of metatranscriptomes to either use direct alignment of sequence reads to reference databases or de novo assembly. As more and more natural environments are covered by metagenomic surveys, this is rapidly changing. This opens up the possibility of genome-resolved studies of prokaryotic metatranscriptomes by mapping to genomes from public repositories or metagenome-assembled genomes derived from the same environment. Here, we present the nf-core/magmap pipeline that provides a reproducible, easy-to-access, and well-documente
papers.summary: This paper introduces nf-core/magmap, a new computational pipeline that enables researchers to map metatranscriptomic sequencing reads directly to large collections of prokaryotic genomes, including both public reference genomes and metagenome-assembled genomes. This approach offers a reproducible and accessible alternative to traditional methods like de novo assembly or direct read alignment to reference databases, facilitating genome-resolved studies of microbial gene expression in natural environments.

papers.title: Application of plant viruses in biotechnology with relation to artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology.
papers.abstract: Plant viruses, once viewed as harmful agricultural pathogens, are now powerful tools in biotechnology. Their nanoscale structure, self-assembly, and biocompatibility enable applications in agriculture, medicine, and environmental sustainability. They serve in gene delivery, genome editing, diagnostics, and nanomaterials for vaccines and drug delivery. Integration with AI, ML, and bioinformatics enhances virus discovery and prediction. Despite challenges, plant viruses are emerging as versatile, sustainable resources for global biotechnological innovations.
papers.summary: This review highlights how plant viruses, traditionally seen as agricultural threats, are being repurposed as versatile tools in biotechnology due to their nanoscale structure and self-assembly properties. The paper discusses their applications in gene delivery, genome editing, diagnostics, and nanomaterials for vaccines and drug delivery, and emphasizes how AI and machine learning can enhance virus discovery and prediction. Despite remaining challenges, plant viruses are emerging as sustainable resources for innovations in agriculture, medicine, and environmental science.

papers.title: NTM-DB: A Comprehensive Non-tuberculosis Mycobacteria Genomic Database.
papers.abstract: Non-tuberculous mycobacteria (NTM) are a major group of environmental bacteria, approximately one-third of which cause serious human infections, particularly respiratory diseases. The global rise in the prevalence and severity of NTM infections has posed a major public health challenge. While high-throughput sequencing has generated vast genomic data on NTM, there remains a lack of comprehensive resources for cross-species genomic analysis. To address these limitations, we developed a specialized database, the Non-tuberculosis Mycobacteria Genomic Database (NTM-DB), tailored for NTM researcher
papers.summary: Researchers created NTM-DB, a comprehensive genomic database for non-tuberculosis mycobacteria (NTM), which are environmental bacteria causing serious human infections. This resource fills a gap by enabling cross-species genomic analysis of NTM, supporting research into the rising global health threat of NTM infections.

papers.title: Cross-definition GWAS of IBS in 2.8 million individuals reveals cardiometabolic and triglyceride-linked mechanisms.
papers.abstract: Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a complex disorder of gut-brain interaction, with heterogeneous symptoms, no available biomarkers and limited pathogenetic insight.
papers.summary: This study conducted a large-scale genome-wide association study (GWAS) of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) across multiple diagnostic definitions in 2.8 million individuals, identifying shared genetic risk factors with cardiometabolic traits and triglyceride levels. The findings suggest that IBS may have underlying mechanisms linked to metabolic health, not just gut-brain interactions, offering new avenues for understanding and treating the condition.

papers.title: Post-GWAS Variant-to-Function Challenges in Asthma Research.
papers.abstract: Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) of asthma have identified nearly 200 independent loci, yet the mechanisms through which individual loci influence asthma risk remain largely unknown. A growing array of computational and experimental tools has begun to fill these gaps by identifying causal variants and effector genes and characterizing their functions. In parallel, emerging studies are exploring the translational applications of genetic and multi-omics data in asthma, including defining molecular endotypes and predicting disease risk. In this manuscript, we review the strengths and limita
papers.journal: The Journal of allergy and clinical immunology
papers.summary: This review discusses the challenge of translating asthma GWAS findings into biological understanding, highlighting computational and experimental tools for identifying causal variants and effector genes. It also explores the translational potential of genetic and multi-omics data for defining asthma endotypes and predicting disease risk.

papers.title: Association of KRTAP24-1 Gene Polymorphisms with Wool Traits in Tibetan Sheep (Ovis aries).
papers.abstract: KRTAP24-1 belongs to the high-sulphur KAP family and has been associated with cashmere fibre diameter in goats, but its role in ovine wool traits remains unclear. This study assessed KRTAP24-1 tissue expression by RT-qPCR and investigated genetic variation and associations with wool traits in 277 Tibetan sheep. Polymorphisms in the coding region were identified by PCR amplification and Sanger sequencing, and genotyping was performed using PARMS. A linear mixed model (LMM) incorporating a genomic relationship matrix (GRM) was used to evaluate associations between SNPs, haplotypes, and 12 wool t
papers.journal: Animals : an open access journal from MDPI
papers.summary: This study investigates the role of the KRTAP24-1 gene in wool traits of Tibetan sheep, finding that specific genetic variants are associated with wool characteristics. The results provide insights into the genetic basis of wool quality, which could inform breeding strategies.

papers.title: MRDsteer: quality-aware AI-driven closed-loop optimization enhances ctDNA-based minimal residual disease detection.
papers.abstract: Accurate identification of ultra-low-frequency tumor-derived variants is critical for circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA)-based minimal residual disease (MRD) profiling. However, current ctDNA analysis workflows largely operate as predefined, sequential pipelines without explicit mechanisms for continuous monitoring of variant-calling performance or adaptive control, thereby limiting detection stability in genomically heterogeneous regions. To address this limitation, we developed MRDsteer, an autonomous closed-loop agent driven by artificial intelligence (AI). MRDsteer monitors the analytical relia
papers.summary: MRDsteer is an AI-driven closed-loop system that continuously monitors and adapts ctDNA analysis to improve the detection of minimal residual disease, addressing the instability of current sequential pipelines in genomically heterogeneous regions.

papers.title: Small Structural Variations, Large Functional Consequences: Comparative Analysis Reveals Structural Control of Ubiquitylation Site Selection by BRCA1/BARD1.
papers.abstract: BRCA1/BARD1 is a chromatin-associated E3 ubiquitin ligase that ubiquitylates histone H2A to coordinate DNA damage repair, transcriptional repression, and genome stability. In Caenorhabditis elegans (C. elegans) , the orthologous BRC-1/BRD-1 com-plex performs analogous functions but exhibits structural variation, most notably through an additional 11-residue loop in BRD-1 that is absent from human BARD1. Prior exper-iments indicate this worm-specific insertion promotes nucleosome engagement and may alter the preferred lysine target for ubiquitylation. Here, we provide a cross-species comparison
papers.journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
papers.summary: This study compares the BRCA1/BARD1 E3 ubiquitin ligase complex between humans and C. elegans, finding that a small structural variation—an 11-residue loop in the worm BRD-1—alters which lysine residue on histone H2A gets ubiquitylated, demonstrating how subtle structural differences can have major functional consequences for genome stability and DNA repair.

papers.title: Integrative serum metabolite prioritization and functional screening identify N-acetyl-L-glutamine as a protective candidate in premature ovarian insufficiency.
papers.abstract: Premature ovarian insufficiency (POI) is a major cause of female infertility and is increasingly associated with systemic metabolic dysregulation. However, whether circulating metabolic alterations contribute causally to POI development or primarily arise as secondary consequences of ovarian failure remains unclear. In this study, bidirectional Mendelian randomization (MR), cell-based screening, and exploratory target-prioritization analyses were integrated to identify POI-related metabolites and functionally relevant candidates.
papers.summary: This study used Mendelian randomization and cell-based screening to identify N-acetyl-L-glutamine as a protective metabolite against premature ovarian insufficiency (POI), suggesting that certain circulating metabolites may causally contribute to POI rather than being mere consequences of ovarian failure. The findings highlight a potential therapeutic candidate for POI and demonstrate an integrative approach for prioritizing functionally relevant metabolites.

papers.title: In-silico analysis of Bifidobacterium bifidum strain 900791 genome in the context of the B. bifidum pangenome.
papers.abstract: Bifidobacterium bifidum is a key member of the human gut microbiota with well-recognised roles in intestinal homeostasis, glycan metabolism, and immunomodulation. Strain 900791, isolated from the meconium of a Siberian infant, has been used as a commercial probiotic ingredient for decades and has demonstrated, in previously published clinical trials, an ability to improve lactose tolerance and reduce gastrointestinal symptoms in both children and adults; however, the genomic basis for these properties has not been characterised.
papers.journal: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology
papers.summary: This study characterizes the genome of the probiotic Bifidobacterium bifidum strain 900791 and places it in the context of the species' pangenome. The analysis reveals genetic features that may explain its beneficial effects on lactose tolerance and gastrointestinal symptoms, providing a genomic basis for its probiotic properties.

papers.title: Real-world genomic profiling of solid tumors: validation and clinical insights from a Brazilian cohort.
papers.abstract: Precision medicine has transformed cancer management by tailoring treatments to the molecular characteristics of a patient's tumor. Comprehensive Genomic Profiling enables the simultaneous analysis of multiple genomic alterations using next-generation sequencing assays, providing detailed molecular profiles of tumors. This approach allows more accurate cancer classification, guides decisions regarding targeted therapies, enhances the precision of treatment strategies, and improves patient outcomes in oncology.
papers.summary: This study validates the use of comprehensive genomic profiling in a Brazilian cohort of solid tumor patients, demonstrating its feasibility and clinical utility in a real-world setting. The findings support the integration of precision oncology approaches in diverse populations, potentially improving treatment decisions and patient outcomes.

papers.title: Integrative genomic and molecular dynamics characterisation of acrB_R717L/Q mutations in azithromycin-resistant Salmonella Typhi from India.
papers.abstract: Azithromycin remains a highly effective oral therapy for uncomplicated typhoid fever caused by extensively drug-resistant (XDR) Salmonella Typhi strains. However, azithromycin resistance has been reported in low and middle-income countries (LMICs). In this study, we identified three azithromycin-resistant S. Typhi isolates and performed phylogenetic analysis along with molecular dynamics simulation to assess the structural stability and conformational dynamics of the acrB efflux pump harbouring R717L/Q mutations.
papers.summary: This study identifies azithromycin-resistant Salmonella Typhi isolates from India carrying R717L/Q mutations in the acrB efflux pump, and uses molecular dynamics simulations to show these mutations alter the pump's structural stability and conformational dynamics. The findings highlight a potential mechanism for emerging azithromycin resistance in typhoid fever, which is concerning for treatment of extensively drug-resistant strains.

papers.title: Genome misassembly detection using Stash: A data structure based on stochastic tile hashing.
papers.abstract: Analyzing large data from high-throughput sequencing technologies presents significant challenges in terms of memory and computational requirements. It is crucial to develop efficient data structures and computational methods that handle sequencing information. These challenges impact bioinformatics studies, including de novo genome assembly, which serves as a foundation of genomics. Issues like read errors or limitations of heuristic decisions in assembly algorithms lead to genome misassemblies and inaccurate genomic representations, compromising the quality of downstream analyses. Hence, de
papers.summary: This paper introduces Stash, a new data structure based on stochastic tile hashing designed to detect misassemblies in genome assemblies. The method efficiently handles large sequencing datasets with reduced memory requirements, helping to identify errors that compromise downstream genomic analyses.

papers.title: Integrative metagenomics and structural bioinformatics identify explainable gut microbial variants associated with Crohn's disease.
papers.abstract: Metagenomics has revealed disease-associated shifts in microbial taxa and functions in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients. However, the role of genomic variation in gut commensals remains poorly understood. Here, we integrated metagenomic profiling, variant calling, and structural bioinformatics to identify disease-associated variants in the gut microbes. Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC) showed significant negative associations with Bacteroides uniformis, Bacteroides vulgatus, and Eubacterium rectale. These bacteria exhibited 190,712 single-nucleotide polymorphisms, incl
papers.summary: This study combines metagenomics and structural bioinformatics to identify specific genetic variants in gut bacteria associated with Crohn's disease, finding that certain species like Bacteroides uniformis are negatively associated with the disease and harbor numerous single-nucleotide polymorphisms that may affect protein function.
